llmResponse.agendaChanges: Board President Weekly proposed moving Item 14.15 (Board Policy 4119.11: Sexual Harassment) from the Consent Calendar to a Discussion item, renumbered as a discussion item considered after Action Items (between Section 12 and Section 14). The item was discussed but no separate vote was taken on it as a pulled item; it was approved as part of the full consent calendar vote. Section 2 (not listed in agenda) was skipped. Sections 11 and 13 had no items to consider. The meeting recording begins at the Reconvene to Open Session (Section 5); no recording of earlier closed session portions.

items.title: Reconvene to Open Session / Roll Call
Report Out on Closed Session from April 1, 2026
Welcome / Pledge of Allegiance
Changes to the Agenda
Approval of the Agenda
Public Comment

publicComments.name: Aida Vázquez
publicComments.summary: A Garfield School parent of two children spoke in Spanish (with translation provided) expressing disagreement with proposed changes to the bilingual program, urging the board to maintain and strengthen the existing bilingual classes rather than making drastic changes.

publicComments.name: Michelle Strode
publicComments.summary: An Orion School parent of a kindergartner and incoming TK student requested that the board expand after-school program availability at Orion to support working families who cannot make use of the TK program without reliable aftercare.

publicComments.name: Sally Dunaway Young
publicComments.summary: A community parent of an incoming Mandarin Immersion TK student thanked the board for expanding the TK program but urged allocation of additional campus space so aftercare providers can expand to serve working families.

publicComments.name: May Liang
publicComments.summary: A parent of an incoming Orion kindergartner who lives in San Carlos and obtained an interdistrict transfer urged the board to add a third classroom for the Play Thrive after-school program to support the Mandarin Immersion community.

publicComments.name: Catherine Stewart
publicComments.summary: A Mandarin Immersion and North Star Academy parent thanked Dr. Baker for his years of support for both programs as he approaches the end of his tenure.

publicComments.name: Josh Hill
publicComments.summary: An online commenter expressed excitement about his child's acceptance to the Orion Mandarin program but requested expansion of after-school care programs to alleviate family anxiety.

publicComments.name: Lilia Amalia Valdez-Monet
publicComments.summary: A Hoover community member since 2017 and classroom volunteer advocated for smaller class sizes, noting that the single Spanish kindergarten class has many high-need students and that reducing class sizes would improve learning and equity.

publicComments.name: Sophia Santos
publicComments.summary: A first grade teacher at Hoover School spoke online about the challenges of large class sizes, noting her class of 27 includes students with IEPs, multilingual learners, and behavioral needs, and urged the board to prioritize manageable class sizes especially in primary grades.

publicComments.name: Kristy Herrera
publicComments.summary: A Hoover kindergarten teacher and lifelong RCSD community member with 34 years of combined experience as student, parent, and educator urged the board to invest in smaller class sizes and equitable distribution of students, warning that the current unequal class sizes are failing to build adequate foundational learning.

items.title: Labor Association Comments

publicComments.name: Maria Stockton
publicComments.summary: CSEA Chapter 5 President introduced herself, acknowledged the Clifford family present, and expressed eagerness to hear the school reports and the CTA presentation, yielding the remainder of her time.
